taxonomy: Urandra corniculata is commonly known as the creeping oxalis. It belongs to the family Oxalidaceae. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions and is also known as the sour grass due to its oxalic acid content. Urandra corniculata is an annual or perennial plant that thrives in areas with partial shade to full sun. Its classification is also aligned with the kingdom Plantae.
properties: Urandra corniculata, also known as Indian knotweed, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various therapeutic properties. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is said to have anti-diabetic and anti-cancer properties, making it a potential treatment for these diseases. Additionally, it is used to treat digestive issues and skin problems due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is required to confirm the efficacy of Urandra corniculata for these uses.
